title: Volunteer campaign planning - Tauranga
description:
**When**: June 08, 2026 at  6:00pm · 2 hrs
**Venue**: Wingates
**Address**: 359 Cameron Rd, Ground floor, Tauranga 3110, New Zealand

![](https://assets.nationbuilder.com/garethmorgan/pages/12401/attachments/original/1779693398/Copy_of_Design_Templates_for_Socials___More_%28Duplicate_Me%29_%282%29.jpg?1779693398)

*Calling all volunteers & supporters!*

If you are interested in volunteering and want to get more involved, then please come along to this campaign planning evening held on **Monday 8th June, at 359 Cameron Road, Wingates 6.00pm - 7.30pm.**

We will run through the different roles, discuss events to attend and go over door knocking.

It's going to be an exciting election, so let's continue to build the momentum around Opportunity.

title: Meet Qiulae Wong and the Canterbury candidates
location: Rolleston Community Centre, Rolleston, New Zealand
description:
**When**: June 09, 2026 at  7:00pm · 2 hrs
**Venue**: Rolleston Community Centre
**Address**: 94 Rolleston Dr, Rolleston 7614, New Zealand

Get excited and ready to meet our candidates based right here in Canterbury.

**RSVP** **below** and **join us** at the **Rolleston Community Centre** to meet the new Opportunity party leader Qiulae Wong. In attendance will be your newly announced candidates, Adrian Mee in Selwyn and Matt Harris in Rangitata. Bolstering the line up is Christchurch Central candidate Eden Skipper. This is a great chance to meet the people standing up for your community, hear their priorities, and ask the questions that matter most to you ahead of the 2026 general election.

Come on down with a friend and make a cuppa. Take a seat and have a listen to our Canterbury team. The floor will then be opened for you to fire back questions.

**7:00pm** Doors open

**7:15pm** Welcoming address

**7:20pm** Keynote speaker Qiulae Wong followed by speeches from your local candidates Adrian, Eden, and Matt.

**8:00pm** Q&A session

For those of you that wish to become part of our volunteer team we have a range of roles that we can use your help with. This could be as simple as leaflet dropping through to phone banking, door knocking or staffing our market tent.

title: Northcote Electorate Catch-up
location: Beer Spot Northcote, Auckland, New Zealand
description:
**When**: June 10, 2026 at  6:00pm · 2 hrs
**Venue**: Beer Spot Northcote
**Address**: 54 Northcote Rd, Northcote, Auckland 0627, New Zealand

We're excited to announce our first Northcote supporters meeting!

Join the candidate, Cody Marsh, for a social get together and casual kōrero. We're planning the campaign and keen to hear your ideas on how we get into parliament in November.

We will be at the [Beer Spot](https://thebeerspot.co.nz/#northcote) between 6pm and 8pm, Wednesday the 10th of June 2026.

title: Meet Robert Mason
location: Sprig & Fern Tawa, Wellington, New Zealand
description:
**When**: June 13, 2026 at  1:00pm · 2 hrs
**Venue**: Sprig & Fern Tawa
**Address**: 99 Main Rd, Tawa, Wellington 5028, New Zealand

**![](https://assets.nationbuilder.com/garethmorgan/pages/12335/attachments/original/1779578017/Wellington_candidate_meeting_flyers.png?1779578017)**

**Introducing Robert Mason - Opportunity Party Candidate for Kenepuru**

We’re delighted to welcome you to meet Robert Mason, the Opportunity Party candidate for Kenepuru.

Robert is an Oxford University graduate, a senior secondary school educator, and a seasoned political strategist. Robert has spent over fifteen years in education using evidence and data to successfully lift student achievement outcomes across diverse communities.

A Wellington local, Robert brings deep practical campaign experience to the race. He previously served as a local electorate chair, managed a successful parliamentary campaign, and stood for the Wellington City Council in 2022.

As Opportunity’s Candidate Manager and Education Policy architect, Robert is an intellectual risk-taker who champions evidence-based, collaborative decision-making.

Robert is ready to bring this unpretentious, future-focused leadership to Parliament to deliver real progress for Kenepuru.

title: Policy & Pints with Kayla Kingdon-Bebb
location: Double Vision Brewing - Miramar, Wellington, New Zealand
description:
**When**: June 13, 2026 at  2:00pm · 2 hrs
**Venue**: Double Vision Brewing - Miramar
**Address**: 128A Unit E, Park Road, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand

Are you Opportunity-curious? Or perhaps you'd like to learn more about our campaign for the Party Vote in Wellington Bays?

Join me for a pint at DvB Miramar this coming Saturday 13 June between 2 and 4pm for a chat about all things policy!

It will also be a chance to meet some of our wonderful campaign team, and to learn how you can get involved in our efforts to make history this coming November.

I look forward to seeing you there!

title: Coffee With Your Candidate
description:

**Address**: 54 Buckingham St, Arrowtown 9302, New Zealand

![](https://assets.nationbuilder.com/garethmorgan/pages/13094/attachments/original/1780359779/Meet_the_candidate_Arrowtown_%283%29.png?1780359779)

Curious about Opportunity Party's plan for the election, and what we'll do once we get into Parliament? Keen to make sure Queenstown's needs are understood? [Bianca Beebe](https://www.opportunity.org.nz/candidate-bianca-beebe) is your local Opportunity Party candidate, working for your Party vote in Southland. She's here to listen.

Come for the coffee, stay for your neighbour's hot political takes :)

title: Meet Qiulae in Mt Albert
description:
**When**: June 14, 2026 at  4:00pm · 1 hr 30 mins
**Venue**: Mt Albert Primary School Hall

**Meet Opportunity Party Leader and Candidate for Mt Albert, Qiulae Wong!**

Qiulae is a Mt Albert local where she lives with her husband Grant and two daughters, one of whom attends Mt Albert Primary where this event will be held.

Come along to hear about the 2026 election campaign for the Opportunity Party, and in particular what this means for the Mt Albert electorate (including Pt Chevalier, Kingsland, Ōwairaka, St Lukes, Morningside, Arch Hill, Grey Lynn, and Westmere).

Qiulae will share her story, why she got into politics, why this is the year for the Opportunity Party and what their priorities are for this election.

We'd love you to come along and share your thoughts on the future of the Mt Albert electorate, where New Zealand is at and where we should be going.

title: Healthy Oceans Casual Forum
location: Invercargill Workingmen's Club, Invercargill, New Zealand
description:
**When**: June 16, 2026 at  6:30pm · 1 hr
**Venue**: Invercargill Workingmen's Club
**Address**: 154 Esk St, Invercargill, Southland 9810, New Zealand

Join us for an introduction to, and discussion about Opportunity Party's Healthy Oceans policy, which covers recreational & industry fishing, governance and marine sustainability.

With guest speaker, party candidate and fishing specialist Derrick Paull, attendees can learn about and ask questions of the policy and the party.

Please RSVP to receive updated information about this event, or otherwise, check back here and show up on the night.

See you there!

title: Nelson Meet your candidate
description:
**When**: June 17, 2026 at  5:30pm · 3 hrs
**Venue**: The Boathouse
**Address**: 326 Wakefield Quay, Nelson 7010, New Zealand

![](https://assets.nationbuilder.com/garethmorgan/pages/13127/attachments/original/1780617628/Meet_Jodie_June_10.png?1780617628)

Jodie Kuntzsch is our new Opportunity Party candidate for the Nelson electorate, and this is your chance to meet her as she launches her campaign.

Be there as it kicks off on Wednesday 17 June — a chance to meet Jodie in person, connect with others from across the community, and be part of a lively, ideas-focused evening about Nelson’s future.

**Date**: Wednesday, 17 June  
**Time**: From 5.30pm (Jodie will speak briefly at 6.15pm)  
**Venue**: The Boathouse, 326 Wakefield Quay, Nelson

Whether you drop in for a quick chat or stay for the evening, you’ll be very welcome. Drinks, snacks and meals will be available to purchase — so bring a friend, bring the whānau, and make a night of it.

title: Palmerston North: Meet your candidate!
location: Palmerston North Community Leisure Centre, Palmerston North, New Zealand
description:
**When**: June 21, 2026 at  2:30pm · 1 hr 30 mins
**Venue**: Palmerston North Community Leisure Centre
**Address**: 569 Ferguson Street Terrace End, Palmerston North 4410, New Zealand

## **Meet Opportunity candidate for Palmerston North, Netra Ghimire, and Party Leader Qiulae Wong!**

Born and raised in a refugee camp in Nepal until the age of 11, Netra is proud to now call New Zealand home. His journey has been shaped by the kindness of everyday Kiwis he has met and a deep desire to give back to the country that gave his family a fresh start.

Netra is a high school teacher of Social Studies, Physical Education, and Financial Literacy, as well as a proud husband and father to an energetic two-year-old. Like many young families, stepping into home ownership opened his eyes to the real pressures everyday New Zealanders are facing - balancing mortgages, childcare, bills, and the rising cost of living despite working hard and spending responsibly.

Working closely with young people every day has shown him both the huge potential within the next generation and the growing disconnect many students feel towards school and their future. Too many young people spend their final years of school simply chasing credits rather than discovering meaningful skills, purpose, confidence, and direction. Netra believes we need stronger pathways into trades, industries, entrepreneurship, technology, and hands-on learning experiences that help students see the value they can bring to the world.

Sport, volunteering, and community have always been central parts of his life. Netra believes NZ’s strength has always come from ordinary people stepping up for one another. He is passionate about building healthier communities, creating stronger opportunities for young people, and bringing more long-term thinking into the way we lead and develop the country.

He believes NZ already has everything it needs to thrive - what we need now is leadership that empowers people, strengthens communities, and unlocks the full potential of Aotearoa New Zealand.

title: Meet Ben and Luke: Candidates for Hutt South and Remutaka
location: Wainuiomata Community Centre, Lower Hutt, New Zealand
description:
**When**: June 28, 2026 at  1:30pm · 2 hrs
**Venue**: Wainuiomata Community Centre
**Address**: Queen Street Wainuiomata, Lower Hutt, Wellington Region 5011, New Zealand

**Meet your Opportunity Party Candidates for Hutt South & Remutaka**

Join us for an engaging **“Meet the Candidates”** event with your Opportunity Party electorate candidates for **Hutt South** and **Remutaka**! This is a great chance to meet the people standing up for your community, hear about their priorities, and ask the questions that matter most ahead of the 2026 general election.

For **Hutt South**, you’ll hear from **Dr Ben Wylie‑van-Eerd** — a career scientist passionate about looking after people in the workplace, and strengthening the New Zealand science system so that our businesses and industries can compete better on the world stage. 

Representing **Remutaka**, **Luke Ross** brings a fresh perspective rooted in his experience in the screen and broadcast industry, community storytelling, and creative communication. A Wellington local with deep ties to the Hutt Valley, Luke is passionate about **rational, evidence‑based politics** that prioritises both environmental wellbeing and local community voices at the decision‑making table.

Whether you’re curious about how the Opportunity Party plans to address housing, climate change, healthcare, or local infrastructure, this event is your opportunity to **connect with Ben and Luke, learn more about their vision, and share your ideas for the future of Hutt South and Remutaka**.

Come along, bring your questions, and help shape the conversation in our communities!
